paper_metadata.title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment
paper_metadata.abstract: Given the broad capabilities of large language models, it should be possible to work towards a general-purpose, text-based assistant that is aligned with human values, meaning that it is helpful, honest, and harmless. As an initial foray in this direction we study simple baseline techniques and evaluations, such as prompting. We find that the benefits from modest interventions increase with model size, generalize to a variety of alignment evaluations, and do not compromise the performance of large models. Next we investigate scaling trends for several training objectives relevant to alignment, comparing imitation learning, binary discrimination, and ranked preference modeling. We find that ranked preference modeling performs much better than imitation learning, and often scales more favorably with model size. In contrast, binary discrimination typically performs and scales very similarly to imitation learning. Finally we study a `preference model pre-training' stage of training, with the goal of improving sample efficiency when finetuning on human preferences.

extraction_data.innovation: The paper introduces a general language assistant aligned using human feedback, focusing on helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness (HHH). It explores preference modeling (PM) and imitation learning (IL), demonstrating that PM scales better for ranked preferences and can be made more sample-efficient via preference model pre-training (PMP) on public datasets like StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia edits.
extraction_data.application: Open-ended natural language dialogue, chat assistants, instruction following, red-teaming for safety, and general language tasks requiring alignment with human values.
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment of large language models to human preferences, specifically improving helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness (HHH) through scalable and sample-efficient methods.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference modeling, imitation learning, context distillation, and reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF)
extraction_data.extension: Preference model pre-training (PMP) stage to improve sample efficiency by leveraging large public datasets encoding human preferences before fine-tuning on specific human feedback datasets.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Decoder-only Transformer models trained with preference model pre-training (PMP) to improve sample efficiency and alignment, leveraging large-scale public datasets like StackExchange, Reddit, and Wikipedia for preference modeling.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Filtered Common Crawl, internet books, and smaller distributions including ~10% Python code data
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks, code generation, summarization, ethics evaluations
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment of large language models using preference modeling and imitation learning
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference model fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning on Python code and other datasets
extraction_data.extension: Models include additional heads for real-valued predictions at all token positions during fine-tuning.

extraction_data.innovation: Introduction of context distillation, a technique to finetune models on a fixed prompt while preserving the conditioning effect of the prompt, improving alignment without significant capability degradation. The paper also explores prompting and preference modeling for alignment, demonstrating improvements in helpfulness, honesty, and harm reduction.
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks, code generation, preference modeling
extraction_data.research_problem: Language model alignment, improving model behavior on helpfulness, honesty, and harm reduction while minimizing alignment tax on model capabilities
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on aligned behavior datasets, including Python code, HHH (Helpful, Honest, Harmless) prompts, and preference modeling datasets
extraction_data.extension: Context distillation: finetuning a model with a KL divergence loss between the prompted model distribution and the finetuned model distribution to preserve prompt conditioning effects while avoiding context window limitations.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.finetuning_task: Context distillation, HHH (Helpfulness, Honesty, Harmlessness) prompting
extraction_data.extension: Context distillation technique to embed long-term memory or pseudo-identity into the model via iterative prompting.
extraction_data.research_problem: Language model alignment and evaluation
extraction_data.application: General-purpose language assistance, chat, instruction following
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Introduction of HHH (Helpful, Honest, Harmless) prompting and context distillation to align language models with human preferences, alongside preference modeling to distinguish 'good' vs 'bad' behavior using ranked samples.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning with HHH (Helpful, Honest, Harmless) prompt
extraction_data.extension: Context distillation technique to compress the HHH prompt into the model's weights, enabling efficient inference without explicit prompting.
extraction_data.research_problem: Language model alignment with human preferences
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, ethical decision-making, code generation
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Code-specific fine-tuning to improve performance on programming tasks while maintaining alignment with HHH principles.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Code-specific fine-tuning with HHH prompt
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment and performance of language models on code generation tasks
extraction_data.application: Code generation, debugging, programming assistance
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Introduction of a three-stage training pipeline: language model pre-training → preference model pre-training (PMP) → preference model fine-tuning. PMP significantly improves sample efficiency when fine-tuning on small alignment-relevant datasets by leveraging large-scale public preference data.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large public preference modeling data (Stack Exchange QA pairs, Reddit comments, Wikipedia edits reverting suspected vandalism)
extraction_data.application: Alignment research, chat assistants, summarization, ethical reasoning, ranked preference tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment of language models via preference modeling and sample-efficient fine-tuning on human feedback data
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference modeling on ranked/binary datasets (e.g., HellaSwag, Learn to Summarize, Ethics)
extraction_data.extension: Preference model pre-training (PMP) phase enables transfer learning across diverse preference modeling datasets, improving performance on small, alignment-specific datasets.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large public preference modeling data (Stack Exchange QA pairs, Reddit comments, Wikipedia edits reverting suspected vandalism)
extraction_data.application: Alignment research, chat assistants, summarization, ethical reasoning, ranked preference tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment of language models via preference modeling and sample-efficient fine-tuning on human feedback data

extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) using binary discrimination datasets derived from ranked preferences, improving sample efficiency in downstream fine-tuning. The method includes a 'binarization' procedure to convert ranked preference data into binary comparisons and incorporates a language modeling loss to imitate 'good' sequences in preference pairs.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with preference modeling
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: StackExchange, Reddit, Wikipedia, and a mixture of all three ('Mix')
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) followed by supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, Ethics evaluations)
extraction_data.extension: Binarization procedure for converting ranked preference datasets into binary discrimination datasets, and appending an end-of-context token to each sequence for preference modeling score prediction.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving sample efficiency and transfer performance in language model alignment through preference modeling pre-training (PMP).
extraction_data.application: Language model alignment, text summarization, commonsense reasoning, ethical judgment tasks, and general language understanding.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Binary pre-training for preference modeling, which establishes a less rigid scoring mechanism compared to ranked pre-training, enabling better transfer to downstream tasks. The paper also explores human-model comparisons for pre-training to improve alignment and transfer performance on model-generated data.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling, Preference Model Pre-training (PMP)
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Reddit, StackExchange, Wikipedia, synthetic data, human-model comparisons
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference modeling, binary and ranked pre-training, imitation learning
extraction_data.extension: Human-model pre-training dataset creation by replacing 'bad' sequences in ranked pairs with model-generated samples, improving transfer on tasks with model-generated data.
extraction_data.research_problem: AI alignment, improving transfer performance of language models using preference modeling and human feedback
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, summarization, ethical evaluations, code correctness, general language tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Introduction of preference modeling pre-training (PMP) using synthetic symbol datasets to improve alignment and sample efficiency in fine-tuning. The paper explores binary vs. ranked PMP and demonstrates robustness in transfer learning across scrambled preference distributions.
extraction_data.application: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks, preference-based ranking of model outputs
extraction_data.research_problem: Alignment of language models with human preferences and improving sample efficiency in fine-tuning
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Preference modeling, discriminative fine-tuning
extraction_data.extension: Per-token GAN-style discriminator trained to distinguish human vs. model-generated tokens, used for re-ranking samples in conjunction with language model probabilities.
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

extraction_data.innovation: Experimental large-scale model to study the impact of Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) on alignment and performance across diverse tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Reddit, Wikipedia, StackExchange, custom datasets (Mix Wiki, UPM datasets)
extraction_data.application: Ethical reasoning, summarization, commonsense reasoning, alignment research
extraction_data.research_problem: Scaling laws for language model alignment and efficient fine-tuning using human preferences
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with Preference Model Pre-training (PMP)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning with human feedback (PMP)
extraction_data.paper_title: A General Language Assistant as a Laboratory for Alignment

properties.label: finetuning_task
properties.value: Preference modeling on ranked/binary datasets (e.g., HellaSwag, Learn to Summarize, Ethics)

properties.label: application
properties.value: Alignment research, chat assistants, summarization, ethical reasoning, ranked preference tasks

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Alignment of language models via preference modeling and sample-efficient fine-tuning on human feedback data

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Introduction of a three-stage training pipeline: language model pre-training → preference model pre-training (PMP) → preference model fine-tuning. PMP significantly improves sample efficiency when fine-tuning on small alignment-relevant datasets by leveraging large-scale public preference data.

properties.label: pretraining_corpus
properties.value: Large public preference modeling data (Stack Exchange QA pairs, Reddit comments, Wikipedia edits reverting suspected vandalism)

properties.label: finetuning_task
properties.value: Preference modeling on ranked/binary datasets (e.g., HellaSwag, Learn to Summarize, Ethics)

properties.label: application
properties.value: Alignment research, chat assistants, summarization, ethical reasoning, ranked preference tasks

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Alignment of language models via preference modeling and sample-efficient fine-tuning on human feedback data

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Introduces Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) using binary discrimination datasets derived from ranked preferences, improving sample efficiency in downstream fine-tuning. The method includes a 'binarization' procedure to convert ranked preference data into binary comparisons and incorporates a language modeling loss to imitate 'good' sequences in preference pairs.

properties.label: pretraining_task
properties.value: Causal language modeling with preference modeling

properties.label: pretraining_corpus
properties.value: StackExchange, Reddit, Wikipedia, and a mixture of all three ('Mix')

properties.label: finetuning_task
properties.value: Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) followed by supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., Learn to Summarize, HellaSwag, Ethics evaluations)

properties.label: application
properties.value: Language model alignment, text summarization, commonsense reasoning, ethical judgment tasks, and general language understanding.

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Improving sample efficiency and transfer performance in language model alignment through preference modeling pre-training (PMP).

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Binary pre-training for preference modeling, which establishes a less rigid scoring mechanism compared to ranked pre-training, enabling better transfer to downstream tasks. The paper also explores human-model comparisons for pre-training to improve alignment and transfer performance on model-generated data.

properties.label: pretraining_task
properties.value: Causal language modeling, Preference Model Pre-training (PMP)

properties.label: pretraining_corpus
properties.value: Reddit, StackExchange, Wikipedia, synthetic data, human-model comparisons

properties.label: finetuning_task
properties.value: Preference modeling, binary and ranked pre-training, imitation learning

properties.label: application
properties.value: Chat, instruction following, summarization, ethical evaluations, code correctness, general language tasks

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: AI alignment, improving transfer performance of language models using preference modeling and human feedback

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Introduction of preference modeling pre-training (PMP) using synthetic symbol datasets to improve alignment and sample efficiency in fine-tuning. The paper explores binary vs. ranked PMP and demonstrates robustness in transfer learning across scrambled preference distributions.

properties.label: application
properties.value: Chat, instruction following, general language tasks, preference-based ranking of model outputs

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Alignment of language models with human preferences and improving sample efficiency in fine-tuning

properties.label: innovation
properties.value: Experimental large-scale model to study the impact of Preference Model Pre-training (PMP) on alignment and performance across diverse tasks.

properties.label: pretraining_task
properties.value: Causal language modeling with Preference Model Pre-training (PMP)

properties.label: pretraining_corpus
properties.value: Reddit, Wikipedia, StackExchange, custom datasets (Mix Wiki, UPM datasets)

properties.label: application
properties.value: Ethical reasoning, summarization, commonsense reasoning, alignment research

properties.label: research_problem
properties.value: Scaling laws for language model alignment and efficient fine-tuning using human preferences
